name: Pic Saillant et Pic du Gar – loop starting from Bezins
name_original: Pic Saillant et Pic du Gar – boucle au départ de Bezins
region_intro: Hiking around Bezins-Garraux, located in the French Pyrenees, offers diverse terrain characterized by majestic peaks, lush forests, and deep valleys. The region is nestled within the natural beauty of the Haut-Garonne Pyrenees, providing access to summits like Pic du Gar and Pic de Cagire. Trails often lead through pleasant forests before ascending to open views, with the Garonne river also flowing through the area. This landscape provides varied hiking opportunities for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Bezins-Garraux The most popular hiking route is Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette Loop from Col de Menté, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the Pyrenees from significant summits. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pic Saillant et Pic du Gar – loop starting from Bezins, a difficult 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail includes a substantial ascent to Pic du Gar, providing expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Escalette Peak – Pic de l'Escalette (pass) loop from Col de Menté, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through mountain passes and offering views of the Pyrenean peaks, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Bezins-Garraux is defined by majestic peaks, lush forests, and the Garonne river valley. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Bezins-Garraux are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 3500 hikers have used komoot to explore Bezins-Garraux's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bezins-Garraux?
A: Bezins-Garraux offers an extensive network of over 110 hiking trails, catering to a wide range of preferences and skill levels. You'll find everything from easy forest walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Bezins-Garraux?
A: The region is characterized by the majestic peaks of the French Pyrenees, including significant summits like Pic du Gar and Pic de Cagire. Trails often lead through lush forests, offering refreshing shade before opening up to panoramic views. The Garonne river also flows through the area, adding to the diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Bezins-Garraux has over 30 easy trails. A good option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26214026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bergerie du Col de Menté – Menté Pass loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5 km long and takes around 1 hour 40 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bezins-Garraux?
A: Many of the trails in Bezins-Garraux are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958331011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette Loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning panoramic views on a 10.8 km circuit.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bezins-Garraux?
A: The hiking trails in Bezins-Garraux are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from tranquil forest paths to exhilarating ridge walks with expansive Pyrenean vistas.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural and scenic landmarks. You might encounter impressive mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet-d'Aspet pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menté Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also lead to significant summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gar Peak&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views.

Q: Can I find trails suitable for families with children?
A: While many trails involve significant elevation, there are easier options that families can enjoy. Look for routes designated as 'easy' in the komoot app, which typically feature less strenuous climbs and shorter distances, often passing through pleasant forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bezins-Garraux?
A: The region offers beautiful hiking experiences throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular for their mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors, with flowering meadows in spring and rich foliage in autumn. During autumn, you might also hear deer during their rutting season. Summer provides warmer conditions for higher altitude routes, while winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and awareness of snow conditions.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails for hikers looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Absolutely. Over 50 trails in Bezins-Garraux are rated as moderate. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41477127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menté Pass – Pic de l'Escalette (pass) loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.9 km route that takes about 2 hours 45 minutes and offers lovely views through mountain passes.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Bezins-Garraux?
A: Access to specific trailheads in the mountainous region around Bezins-Garraux often requires a car. While there might be limited local bus services to larger villages, direct public transport to many remote starting points for hikes is generally not extensive. It's advisable to check local transport schedules or plan for car access.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Garonne river?
A: Yes, the Garonne river flows through the area, and some trails will offer glimpses or follow parts of its course. The region's diverse landscape includes deep valleys where the river and its network of streams are prominent features, providing opportunities to observe this significant waterway.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse natural habitats of Bezins-Garraux support a variety of wildlife. Hikers might spot deer, especially during autumn, and numerous bird species. The area's rich flora, including flowering meadows, also contributes to a vibrant ecosystem, making it a rewarding experience for nature lovers.
tips: Often confused with the Pic du Gar. They are brothers and each will offer you a superb panorama.

This peak must be climbed when you come to the Frontignes. To have the chance to observe the sunrises and sunsets there is... Do not hesitate to stop at the refuge at the exit of the wood just before climbing the mountain pasture.
The Col de Caude, accessible from Boutx or Bezins, offers lovely views and provides access to the Pic du Gar to the west and to the Pic de l'Escalette and then to the Pic de Gagire to the east.
